sourceMovie.title: Total Recall
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Total Recall (1990) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Total Recall clicked for you, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Total Recall clicked for you, The Matrix Resurrections (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Total Recall?
faq.answer: Try Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials, The Matrix Resurrections, Bloodshot—each shares DNA with Total Recall while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Total Recall?
faq.answer: Total Recall (1990) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Total Recall worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
